description: Structured negative capabilities. Every non-claim is enumerated, categorized, and justified. This is the build roadmap: knowing what doesn't work is how we plan what to build next.

categories.label: Permanent Non-Claims
categories.desc: These will never be claimed. They are design boundaries, not gaps.

items.claim: Not a GNU m4 replacement until all courts sealed
items.justification: Replacement claim requires 100% surface parity. This is the terminal goal, not a deferral.
m4 is a macro processor, not a sandbox. syscmd/esyscmd execute arbitrary commands by design. This matches GNU m4 behavior.
GNU m4 is byte-oriented (eight-bit-clean). m4-rs follows this design: core operates on bytes, not Unicode.
Byte-exact behavior is the target, not wall-clock performance. Performance may be better or worse.
Different build systems (autotools vs cargo). Output behavior is the only comparandum.

categories.label: Profile-Bound Non-Claims
categories.desc: True for some m4 variants, not portable claims.

items.claim: Vendor m4 parity not claimed from GNU m4 parity
items.justification: A receipt against gnu_m4_1_4_20_default does not imply busybox_m4, bsd_m4, or system_m4 parity. Each is a separate profile.
GNU m4 extends POSIX significantly. Posix-mode requires separate admission.
Autoconf is a framework built on m4, not just macro expansion. Autoconf survival is a separate parity ladder.

categories.label: Deferred Non-Claims
categories.desc: Will be claimed when dependencies are sealed. Each has a specific dependency chain.

items.justification: Depends on: macro table (M4.DEFINE.1), diversion system (M4.DIVERT.1), quote/comment config serialization, pushdef stack serialization, version mismatch handling. Cannot be sealed before Phase 10.
Depends on: sandboxed test infrastructure, child process management, exit code capture, stderr passthrough semantics. syscmd/esyscmd/sysval all depend on each other.

Depends on: i18n decisions (15 language translations), oracle version stability, errprint/__file__/__line__/m4exit implementation, full diagnostic taxonomy. Byte-exact diagnostic matching is the most version-sensitive surface.
Will be tested in hostile/fuzz phases. Requires: recursion limit, input size limits, diversion buffer limits, panic-free error paths.
Multi-stage ladder: seed macros → macro files → autom4te invocation → configure generation → configure execution. Depends on virtually all builtins being sealed first.
Depends on: full expansion engine, line tracking, debug flags, dumpdef, output redirection.

categories.label: Unimplemented Surfaces
categories.desc: Not yet started. These are the active implementation targets.

builds_on: understanding of all POSIX vs GNU differences

critical_implementation_sequence: Wire input stack, lexer, engine together
